title: Largest cities in the Americas

infobox.value: [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") |  | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") | 8,478,072[[8]](#cite_note-:1-10) | 2024
[Santa Cruz de la Sierra](/wiki/Santa_Cruz_de_la_Sierra "Santa Cruz de la Sierra") |  | [Bolivia](/wiki/Bolivia "Bolivia") | 1,606,671[[32]](#cite_note-2024-BOL-CENSUS-39) | 2024

by population residing [within city limits](/wiki/City_proper "City proper") based on the most recent official census results, estimates, or short-term projections available for all of these cities, which refer to mid-2020 populations. These figures do not reflect the population of the [urban agglomeration](/wiki/Urban_agglomeration "Urban agglomeration") or [metropolitan area](/wiki/Metropolitan_area "Metropolitan area") which typically do not coincide with the administrative boundaries of the city. For a list of the latter, see *[List of metropolitan areas in the Americas by population](/wiki/List_of_metropolitan_areas_in_the_Americas_by_population "List of metropolitan areas in the Americas by population")*. These figures refer to mid-2020 populations with the following exceptions:

While there is no official administrative definition of the city, it is generally considered to consist of the central 30 of the 43 districts of [Lima Province](/wiki/Lima_Province "Lima Province"), corresponding to the urban area centered around the historic Cercado de Lima district.
Population estimate is that of the federal entity known since 2016 as *Ciudad de México* and previously as the *Distrito Federal*.[[1]](#cite_note-MXdate-1)

Population projection is that of the Metropolitan District of Caracas, which comprises the municipalities of [Libertador](/wiki/Libertador_Bolivarian_Municipality "Libertador Bolivarian Municipality"), [Chacao](/wiki/Chacao,_Venezuela "Chacao, Venezuela"), [Baruta](/wiki/Baruta "Baruta"), [Sucre](/wiki/Sucre,_Miranda "Sucre, Miranda") and [El Hatillo](/wiki/El_Hatillo_Municipality,_Venezuela "El Hatillo Municipality, Venezuela").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Largest cities in the Americas](/w/index.php?title=Largest_cities_in_the_Americas&redirect=no "Largest cities in the Americas"))

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article needs to be **updated**. Please help update this article to reflect recent events or newly available information. *(August 2025)* |

This is a list of the 50 **largest cities in the [Americas](/wiki/Americas "Americas")** by population residing [within city limits](/wiki/City_proper "City proper") based on the most recent official census results, estimates, or short-term projections available for all of these cities, which refer to mid-2020 populations. These figures do not reflect the population of the [urban agglomeration](/wiki/Urban_agglomeration "Urban agglomeration") or [metropolitan area](/wiki/Metropolitan_area "Metropolitan area") which typically do not coincide with the administrative boundaries of the city. For a list of the latter, see *[List of metropolitan areas in the Americas by population](/wiki/List_of_metropolitan_areas_in_the_Americas_by_population "List of metropolitan areas in the Americas by population")*. These figures refer to mid-2020 populations with the following exceptions:

1. Mexican cities, whose figures derive from the 2015 Intercensal Survey conducted by [INEGI](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Statistics_and_Geography "National Institute of Statistics and Geography") with a reference date of March 15, 2020;[[1]](#cite_note-MXdate-1)
2. Chilean cities, whose figures derive from the 2017 Census projection by [Instituto Nacional de Estadísticas](/wiki/National_Statistics_Institute_(Chile) "National Statistics Institute (Chile)");[[2]](#cite_note-Chile-2)
3. Venezuelan cities, whose figures originate from the 2015 estimate given by the Instituto Nacional de Estadística, with a reference date of 2016.[[3]](#cite_note-Venezuela2-3)

|  | City | Image | Country | Population | Year |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo") |  | [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") | 11,904,961[[4]](#cite_note-4) | 2025 |
| 2 | [Lima](/wiki/Lima "Lima") |  | [Peru](/wiki/Peru "Peru") | 10,092,000[[a]](#cite_note-5)[[5]](#cite_note-2023-PER-INEI-6) | 2023 |
| 3 | [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City") |  | [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") | 9,209,944[[b]](#cite_note-7)[[6]](#cite_note-EI-8)[[7]](#cite_note-citypop-9) | 2021 |
| 4 | [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") |  | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") | 8,478,072[[8]](#cite_note-:1-10) | 2024 |
| 5 | [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá") |  | [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") | 7,862,277[[c]](#cite_note-Colombia-12)[[10]](#cite_note-2022-COL-DANE-13) | 2024 |
| 6 | [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro") |  | [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") | 6,729,894[[11]](#cite_note-14) | 2024 |
| 7 | [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago") |  | [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile") | 7,400,741[[12]](#cite_note-15) | 2024 |
| 8 | [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") |  | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") | 3,878,704[[13]](#cite_note-:0-16) | 2024 |
| 9 | [Caracas](/wiki/Caracas "Caracas") |  | [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela") | 3,289,886[[d]](#cite_note-17)[[14]](#cite_note-Venezuela-18) | 2015 |
| 10 | [Buenos Aires](/wiki/Buenos_Aires "Buenos Aires") |  | [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina") | 3,121,707[[15]](#cite_note-2022-ARG-CENSUS-19) | 2022 |
| 11 | [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto") |  | [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") | 3,025,657[[16]](#cite_note-20) | 2021 |

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_largest_cities_in_the_Americas&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** While there is no official administrative definition of the city, it is generally considered to consist of the central 30 of the 43 districts of [Lima Province](/wiki/Lima_Province "Lima Province"), corresponding to the urban area centered around the historic Cercado de Lima district.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-7)** Population estimate is that of the federal entity known since 2016 as *Ciudad de México* and previously as the *Distrito Federal*.[[1]](#cite_note-MXdate-1)
3. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Colombia_12-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Colombia_12-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-Colombia_12-2) [***d***](#cite_ref-Colombia_12-3) Population projections for Colombian cities are that of the *cabecera municipal* (municipal seat).[[9]](#cite_note-11)
4. **[^](#cite_ref-17)** Population projection is that of the Metropolitan District of Caracas, which comprises the municipalities of [Libertador](/wiki/Libertador_Bolivarian_Municipality "Libertador Bolivarian Municipality"), [Chacao](/wiki/Chacao,_Venezuela "Chacao, Venezuela"), [Baruta](/wiki/Baruta "Baruta"), [Sucre](/wiki/Sucre,_Miranda "Sucre, Miranda") and [El Hatillo](/wiki/El_Hatillo_Municipality,_Venezuela "El Hatillo Municipality, Venezuela").
5. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-MXcabecera_31-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-MXcabecera_31-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-MXcabecera_31-2) [***d***](#cite_ref-MXcabecera_31-3) [***e***](#cite_ref-MXcabecera_31-4) Population estimate is that of the *[cabecera municipal](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Mexico#Structure "Municipalities of Mexico")* (municipal seat).
6. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-MXdate_33-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-MXdate_33-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-MXdate_33-2) [***d***](#cite_ref-MXdate_33-3) [***e***](#cite_ref-MXdate_33-4) [***f***](#cite_ref-MXdate_33-5) [***g***](#cite_ref-MXdate_33-6) Population estimate has a reference date of 2023.[[1]](#cite_note-MXdate-1)
7. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-MXmunicipio_36-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-MXmunicipio_36-1) Population estimate is that of the entire *[municipio](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Mexico "Municipalities of Mexico")*.
